problem: Aurora audio needs a formal contract layer so ingestion, candidate generation, runtime logging, comparison, promotion, and theme variation all optimize the same player-facing meaning rather than isolated waveform similarity.
strategy: Load the audio cue contracts, join them to application guide mappings, event-gap risks, focused candidate reports, promotion prechecks, and current quality scoring, then identify which cue families need stronger evidence or a different generation strategy.
successMeasure: A cue contract is ready for runtime work when semantic meaning, reference grounding, runtime evidence, and theme latitude all score strongly, and runtime promotion remains blocked until live recapture holds the full quality gates.

cues.playerMeaning: The alien rack is alive, organized, and exerting pressure even when no shot or dive is occurring.

eventShape.trigger: starts after formation arrival and repeats during standard-stage play
eventShape.cooldownPolicy: cadence must preserve pressure without masking shots, hits, or transition cues
eventShape.overlapPolicy: must duck or stop for stageTransition, challengeTransition, challengeResults, and challengePerfect

currentRisk.recommendation: Keep as secondary pass after higher-risk audio gaps are narrowed.

candidate.reason: No Formation Pulse candidate cleared whole-cue, segment, duration, band, centroid, and role-match gates.

blockers: No Formation Pulse candidate cleared whole-cue, segment, duration, band, centroid, and role-match gates.

cues.recommendation: Do not promote refclip-s540-d300-v105; the latest stagePulse pass improved local risk but still failed masking/stability gates. Build a lower-brightness, lower-variance pressure-bed family before another runtime trial.
nextLearningQuestions: Is the reference window too narrow for formation pressure?
Do we need a ducking/mix model before tuning tone?

cues.playerMeaning: A shot connected but may not have destroyed the target.

eventShape.trigger: fires immediately on standard enemy hit
eventShape.cooldownPolicy: short cooldown; should tolerate rapid shot cadence
eventShape.overlapPolicy: may overlap lightly with enemyBoom only if hit/kill distinction remains legible

variantAllowances: small pitch/gain shifts by enemy family

currentRisk.recommendation: Prioritize impact/explosion event clarity so the player understands damage, kill, and boss multi-hit state.

candidate.reason: No Enemy Hit candidate cleared whole-cue, segment, duration, band, centroid, and role-match gates.

cues.recommendation: Keep this cue as a guardrail while higher-risk audio contracts are improved.
nextLearningQuestions: Do boss/multi-hit enemies need a separate damage layer?

cues.playerMeaning: A normal enemy was destroyed and points were earned.

eventShape.cooldownPolicy: short enough for dense challenge-stage kills
eventShape.overlapPolicy: can overlap with score feedback, but must not bury playerShot or playerHit

variantAllowances: enemy-family color and small gain scaling

currentRisk.recommendation: Prioritize impact/explosion event clarity so the player understands damage, kill, and boss multi-hit state.

candidate.reason: No Enemy Boom candidate cleared whole-cue, segment, duration, band, centroid, and role-match gates.

cues.recommendation: Keep this cue as a guardrail while higher-risk audio contracts are improved.
nextLearningQuestions: Should challenge-stage dense kills use a lower gain or grouped limiter?

cues.playerMeaning: A boss or stronger target took damage but may still be alive.

eventShape.overlapPolicy: should not mask bossBoom or capture cues

currentRisk.recommendation: Prioritize impact/explosion event clarity so the player understands damage, kill, and boss multi-hit state.

candidate.reason: No Boss Hit candidate cleared whole-cue, segment, duration, band, centroid, and role-match gates.

cues.recommendation: Keep this cue as a guardrail while higher-risk audio contracts are improved.
nextLearningQuestions: Should visual damage flash and bossHit audio share a timing contract?

cues.playerMeaning: A boss was destroyed; this should sound more consequential than a normal kill.

eventShape.overlapPolicy: can dominate nearby enemyBoom but not playerHit or capture/rescue state changes

currentRisk.recommendation: Prioritize impact/explosion event clarity so the player understands damage, kill, and boss multi-hit state.

candidate.reason: No Boss Boom candidate cleared whole-cue, segment, duration, band, centroid, and role-match gates.

cues.recommendation: Keep this cue as a guardrail while higher-risk audio contracts are improved.
nextLearningQuestions: Does bossBoom need a score-surface synchronization marker?

cues.playerMeaning: The captured fighter was rescued and rejoined; this is a major positive state change.

eventShape.cooldownPolicy: long; should not retrigger during the same rescue sequence
eventShape.overlapPolicy: must not be confused with challengePerfect or capturedFighterDestroyed

currentRisk.recommendation: Keep as secondary pass after higher-risk audio gaps are narrowed.

candidate.reason: Rescue Join candidate clears measured segment keeper gates.

wins: Theme average worst-segment risk improves by 0.18/10.

runtimeTrial.reason: Focused and precheck evidence looked strong, but the measured runtime trial did not improve rescueJoin in the live full-theme event-gap report and worsened aggregate/highest audio risk; runtime cue was reverted.
runtimeTrial.nextStep: Do not retry this rescueJoin subwindow directly. Use the audio risk stability report to require repeated or median full-theme confirmation before runtime promotion, then return to challengePerfect/gameOver only after capture volatility is controlled.

cues.recommendation: Do not retry this rescueJoin subwindow directly. Use the audio risk stability report to require repeated or median full-theme confirmation before runtime promotion, then return to challengePerfect/gameOver only after capture volatility is controlled.
nextLearningQuestions: Can reference subclip promotion work, or should this stay a synthesized arcade reward motif?

cues.playerMeaning: The player lost a ship; this should be unmistakable and emotionally negative.

eventShape.trigger: fires immediately on player ship destruction
eventShape.cooldownPolicy: long; one cue per loss event
eventShape.overlapPolicy: must dominate enemyBoom/bossBoom but not smear into gameOver or next-life restart

variantAllowances: small timbre changes by theme if segment roles remain clear

currentRisk.recommendation: Keep playerHit as a calibrated guardrail for now: raw body/tail risk matches synthetic Galaga browser-capture behavior, so the next runtime work should target a non-calibrated cue gap.

candidate.reason: No Ship Loss Tail/Body candidate cleared whole-cue, calibrated role/segment, duration, band, centroid, and role-match gates.

runtimeTrial.reason: Focused ship-loss candidate and promotion precheck were strong, but the measured runtime trial lowered the audio category score and did not resolve the full-theme active body/tail segmentation gap.
runtimeTrial.nextStep: Build a contract-aware playerHit candidate/pass that either separates death onset/body/tail into runtime cue phases or improves the scoring/capture strategy so the active body is evaluated against the intended sub-event.

cues.recommendation: Build a contract-aware playerHit candidate/pass that either separates death onset/body/tail into runtime cue phases or improves the scoring/capture strategy so the active body is evaluated against the intended sub-event.
nextLearningQuestions: Should the player death implementation become a scheduled multi-part cue rather than one clipped sample?

cues.playerMeaning: The player perfectly cleared a challenge stage; this is special and should exceed the standard result cue.

eventShape.trigger: fires shortly after perfect challenge clear
eventShape.overlapPolicy: must stop or avoid stagePulse and challengeTransition; must leave clean handoff to next stage

variantAllowances: theme-colored flourish if timing and result hierarchy remain

currentRisk.recommendation: Tune the onset segment first: onset reference sub-event is not separated in runtime; the cue is collapsing multiple reference moments into one continuous segment.

candidate.reason: Challenge Perfect candidate clears measured calibrated role/segment keeper gates.

wins: Theme average worst-segment risk improves by 0.17/10.

blockers: Global audio scoring is unstable (19/21 cues volatile; most volatile captureBeam 3.89/10), so runtime promotion is blocked until scorer/capture stability improves.
warnings: Cue is volatile across repeated full-theme reads (2.49/10 range); single-run wins must clear the noise floor.
wins: Cue gap win 6.71/10 clears 1.88/10 stability threshold.

runtimeTrial.reason: Challenge Perfect runtime trial rejected: the focused/stability-gated candidate improved the average worst-segment rollup, but live full-theme recapture worsened Challenge Perfect itself to 7.7/10 as the highest audio risk, so this is preserved as generator evidence and not promoted.
runtimeTrial.nextStep: Do not promote Challenge Perfect from isolated onset/body candidates. Replace the next audio strategy with full-phrase/segment-boundary work: stabilize the scorer on canonical reference-vs-reference capture, then generate candidates that optimize onset, body, tail, and live capture segmentation together.

cues.recommendation: Do not promote Challenge Perfect from isolated onset/body candidates. Replace the next audio strategy with full-phrase/segment-boundary work: stabilize the scorer on canonical reference-vs-reference capture, then generate candidates that optimize onset, body, tail, and live capture segmentation together.
nextLearningQuestions: Do we need separate perfect-result scoring for audio and score-surface animation?

processPosition: The audio process now separates contract readiness from runtime promotion. Current evidence says contracts can guide the next candidate cycle, but no cue should ship from synthetic or focused evidence alone.
nextStep: Do not promote Challenge Perfect from isolated onset/body candidates. Replace the next audio strategy with full-phrase/segment-boundary work: stabilize the scorer on canonical reference-vs-reference capture, then generate candidates that optimize onset, body, tail, and live capture segmentation together.
